repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Documents, videos, and film from Phil Zwickler's work on "Rights and Reactions," as well as several other projects, including some material regarding "Fear of Disclosure," and miscellaneous files and photographs, mostly from 1987 to 1992.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Filmmaker and writer about gay and lesbian issues and the AIDS crisis, born June 1, 1954 and died from the complications of AIDS on May 7, 1991. With Jane Lippman, he produced and directed "Rights and Reactions: Lesbian and Gay Rights on Trial," an award-winning documentary covering the 1986 New York City Council hearings on the gay rights bill. Zwickler collaborated with David Wojnarowicz to produce "Fear of Disclosure," a short film about the issues gay men face dating in the context of AIDS. Zwickler also worked with experimental German film maker, Rosa von Praunheim, to produce "Silence Equals Death" and "Positive," both exploring the emotions and anger of people with AIDS. The film "Positive" includes interviews with Zwickler and two other men who were HIV-positive. He produced his final project, "The Needle Nightmares" (1991), with the help of David Meieran. Zwickler also worked as a news reporter, publishing stories in the Village Voice and serving as editor of the People with AIDS Coalition Newsline for a number of years. Before working in film and journalism, he taught literature in New York City public schools and community colleges.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents, publicity, funding proposals, correspondence, production notes, and video outtakes from Phil Zwickler's work on "Rights and Reactions," as well as several other documentary and video projects, including "Fear of Disclosure," "Silence = Death," and "Positive." Also, documentation of his work as editor for "PWA Coalition Newsline," and miscellaneous files and photographs, mostly from 1987 to 1992.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Mireille Lee, March 2000Peter MartinezSarah Keen, July 2008Sarah Keen, March 2010

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: 29 tape recordings that are part of the "Rights and Reactions" project and may only be screened or viewed for research, scientific, or scholarly purposes. Any and all uses other than the aforementioned are expressly prohibited. No duplication, sale, rental, broadcast, or public screening is authorized

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. All magnetic media in series II has been digitized.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Phil Zwickler papers, #7564. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Resume, Personal narrative, and proposals for "Fear of disclosure"
Memorial service items and obituaries - collected by archivistPhotographs, contact sheets, negatives
"[Canada?]/LA PZ 8/89," including photos of mountains, urban centers, Zwickler with a chiropractor, an ACT UP meeting, national lesbian and gay rights activist Urvashi Vaid, etc.
"Edison #1001," an ACT UP rally in New York City including photographs of arrests and of artist Keith Haring
Letter to Allen, Caren and Parents; also pictures and a drawing
Agreements with Zwickler about "Rights and Reactions," also records about Realis; involvement with Zwickler's memorial service
Memos of agreement, correspondence, etc. with Realis
Financial records: New York State Council on the Arts
Rights and Reactions sticker and invitation
Technicals, content, production and post-production, miscellaneous
Festival International du Film Documentaire Nyon
NY Lesbian and Gay Experimental Film Festival
Correspondence, clippings and publicity in folder labeled "To Dad"
Letter, Owen Levy to [Allen or Seymour] Zwickler
"Fear of Disclosure": Notes on script and production
"Fear of Disclosure": "(In)visible Women" and other work of the Fear of Disclosure project
"Images and Realities: Towards New Cultural Initiatives in the Lesbian and Gay Community," plans for a retreat
PWA Coalition Newsline, Phil Zwickler editor
NY Gay and Lesbian Film Festival Catalog
Info on AIDS in NYC that PZ had inserted in the Program for GMHC's Ringling Bros. Fundraising Program
Part of a publication by P.P.O.W., art, poetry, photographs
Cover for a record (LP) by Somerville/Steinbacheck/Bronski
Clippings on AIDS treatments, research and government response
"Fear of Disclosure" letters of support, etc.
"Fear" at NY International Festival of Lesbian and Gay Film
"Fear" at San Francisco Lesbian and Gay Film Festival and at Los Angeles Gay and Lesbian Film and Video Festival
Wojnarowicz: "Target City Hall: An AIDS Guide to NYC in 1989"
Phil's Positive Diary and other materials re: Positive
"Silence = Death", "Positive", and "Fear and Disclosure": reviews and publicity
Application to NYSCA for Distribution of Film: "Silence = Death"
